codeReview.summary: The fix correctly moves the @admin regex compilation from per-message to package-level initialization. The regex pattern `(?i)@admin(s)?` is now compiled once at module init time using `regexp.MustCompile`, stored in the `adminMentionRegex` package variable, and reused by the message handler. This eliminates O(n) compilation overhead becoming O(1).

summary: Fix is correctly implemented. The regex is compiled once at module init using `var adminMentionRegex = regexp.MustCompile(

)`, eliminating per-message compilation overhead. The handler now uses the pre-compiled regex via `adminMentionRegex.MatchString(msg.Text)`. Tests pass with race detection enabled. The change is minimal, idiomatic Go, and introduces no functional changes - only performance improvement.
